title: Podman pull - add policy flag
body:
#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
Yes

```release-note
Add policy argument to image pull 
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

I moved the tests from local integration to system in order to use an additional registry. Since there was no general file for pull, I created a new one. LMK if I should rename it or give it a different number

> I don't think this works via remote/API

The `PullPolicy` flag already existed in the ImagePullOptions struct: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/7808625785b13d07b1e2005f7510b7b61a5d3dc7/pkg/domain/entities/images.go#L116 since it is used for `podman run` and it is copied in the `tunnel` implementation already: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/7808625785b13d07b1e2005f7510b7b61a5d3dc7/pkg/domain/infra/tunnel/images.go#L112

Having said that, I'm not sure how to add a CI test for it in a remote case because the test needs to run with a registry and it is running on localhost

Oh you are right, I wasn't aware the pull endpoint and bindings already implemented the policy option and just assumed they weren't without actually looking, my bad.

I guess once option to at least have some remote coverage is to still do 
`run_podman 125 pull --policy never quay.io/libpod/i-do-not-exist` and then ensure you get the proper "image not known" message
That does not require a local registry to be running and makes sure we did not try to talk to the registry
I've added the test and moved the skip and starting of the registry to the specific test
